overview.short: Panimur is a scenic destination in the Dima Hasao district of Assam, India.
overview.description: Panimur is a scenic destination in the Dima Hasao district of Assam, India. It is most famous for the Panimur Waterfall, also known as Niagara of Northeast, which forms from the Kopili River as it flows over rocky terrain, creating a wide, cascading waterfall that is a major attraction for tourists and photographers.

topPlaces.name: Diyungbra
topPlaces.description: Diyungbra (IPA: Di-yung-bra [Diyung means Big river and bra means branch], etymological, River mouth of Diyung ; place where river Diyung and Kopili meets) is a newly created Sub-Division and an ITD Block in Dima Hasao district in the Indian state of Assam. Diyungbra is also one of the three sub-divisions of Dima Hasao District.

items.note: Diyungbra (IPA: Di-yung-bra [Diyung means Big river and bra means branch], etymological, River mouth of Diyung ; place where river Diyung and Kopili meets) is a newly created Sub-Division and an ITD Block in Dima Hasao district in the Indian state of Assam. Diyungbra is also one of the three sub-divisions of Dima Hasao District.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Panimur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Panimur is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

The nearest airport is Shillong Airport (Umroi) (85 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (93 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Panimur?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Panimur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Panimur?
faq.a: Stays in Panimur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
